sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47
sources.population: People with muscle sprains or strains suitable for virtual assessment.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for sprain- or strain-compatible injury documentation in Dubai telehealth; partial for broad foot, hand, sports, fracture, operative, or diagnosis-specific workflows.

exact_sections.heading: 1–6 Background, scope, applicability, causes, and risk factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

exact_sections.heading: 7 Symptoms and signs; 7.4 Relevant history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

exact_sections.heading: 8 Red flags; 9 Differential diagnosis; 10 Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

exact_sections.heading: 11 Management; 12 Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.
